summary: This command is used to draw a button with a UTF-8 label

params.name: x
params.desc: X-coordinate of button top-left, in pixels

params.name: y
params.desc: Y-coordinate of button top-left, in pixels

params.name: options
params.desc: OPT_3D - default option, the button is drawn with a 3D effect. OPT_FLAT - removes the 3D effect. OPT_FORMAT - processes the text as a format string. OPT_FILL - breaks the text at spaces into multiple lines, with maximum width set by CMD_FILLWIDTH. Note that if this text string is placed at the crossing area of the RAM_CMD 16 Kbytes region and this OPT_FILL and CMD_FILLWIDTH are used, there will be corruption of the text. This can be fixed with a patch.

params.name: s
params.desc:
Button label. It must be one string terminated with NUL character, i.e. '\0' in C language. UTF-8 encoded. If OPT_FILL is not given then the string may contain newline (
) characters, indicating line breaks

OPT_CASESENSITIVE if enabled, filename comparison is case sensitive. OPT_DIRSEP_WIN treat backslash as a directory path separator. OPT_DIRSEP_UNIX treat forward slash as a directory path separator

params.name: size

params.name: height
params.desc: The height of the image which is decoded by the last CMD_LOADIMAGE before this command

name: EVE_CoCmd_getPtr
signature: bool EVE_CoCmd_getPtr(EVE_HalContext *phost, uint32_t *result)
summary: This command returns the current allocation pointer. The allocation pointer is advanced by the following commands: cmd_inflate, cmd_loadasset, cmd_loadimage, cmd_loadwav, cmd_playvideo, cmd_playwav, cmd_videoframe, cmd_endlist

params.name: result
params.desc: Pointer to the first unallocated memory location

name: EVE_CoCmd_glow
signature: void EVE_CoCmd_glow(EVE_HalContext *phost, int16_t x, int16_t y, int16_t w, int16_t h)
summary: The glow command draws an additive glow effect centered in a rectangle, using the current color

params.name: x
params.desc: x-coordinate of top-left of glow rectangle, in pixels

params.name: y
params.desc: y-coordinate of top-left of glow rectangle, in pixels

params.name: w
params.desc: width of glow rectangle in pixels

params.name: h
params.desc: height of glow rectangle in pixels

name: EVE_CoCmd_gradColor
signature: void EVE_CoCmd_gradColor(EVE_HalContext *phost, uint32_t c)
summary: This command is used to set the 3D Button Highlight Color

params.name: c
params.desc: New highlight gradient color, as a 24-bit RGB number. White is the default value, i.e., 0xFFFFFF. Red is the most significant 8 bits, blue is the least. So 0xFF0000 is bright red. Gradient is supported only for Button and Keys widgets

name: EVE_CoCmd_gradient
signature: void EVE_CoCmd_gradient(EVE_HalContext *phost, int16_t x0, int16_t y0, uint32_t rgb0, int16_t x1, int16_t y1, uint32_t rgb1)
summary: This command is used to draw a smooth color gradient

params.name: rgb0
params.desc: Color of point 0, as a 24-bit RGB number. Red is the most significant 8 bits, Blue is the least. So 0xff0000 is bright red

params.name: rgb1
params.desc: Color of point 1, as a 24-bit RGB number. Red is the most significant 8 bits, Blue is the least. So 0xff0000 is bright red

name: EVE_CoCmd_gradientA
signature: void EVE_CoCmd_gradientA(EVE_HalContext *phost, int16_t x0, int16_t y0, uint32_t argb0, int16_t x1, int16_t y1, uint32_t argb1)
summary: This command is used to draw a smooth color gradient with transparency

params.name: argb0
params.desc: color of point 0, as a 32-bit ARGB number. A is the most significant 8 bits, B is the least. So 0x80ff0000 is 50% transparent bright red, and 0xff0000ff is solid blue.

params.name: argb1
params.desc: color of point 1, as a 32-bit ARGB number. A is the most significant 8 bits, B is the least. So 0x80ff0000 is 50% transparent bright red, and 0xff0000ff is solid blue.

name: EVE_CoCmd_graphicsFinish
signature: void EVE_CoCmd_graphicsFinish(EVE_HalContext *phost)
summary: The command waits until the render engine is idle

name: EVE_CoCmd_i2sStartup
signature: void EVE_CoCmd_i2sStartup(EVE_HalContext *phost, uint32_t freq)
summary: The I2S startup command fills the I2S output FIFO to the halfway mark with zeroes, writes the given frequency to REG_I2S_FREQ and enables I2S by writing 1 to REG_I2S_EN

name: EVE_CoCmd_inflate
signature: void EVE_CoCmd_inflate(EVE_HalContext *phost, uint32_t ptr, uint32_t options)
summary: This command is used to decompress the following compressed data into RAM_G. The data may be supplied in the command buffer, the media FIFO, or from flash memory or from SD card

params.name: ptr
params.desc: Destination address. If -1 is given as the address, then the current allocation pointer is used instead

params.name: options
params.desc: By default, the data is sourced from the command buffer, immediately following the command itself. Other sources can be selected using one of the following options: OPT_FLASH : flash memory is the source; OPT_FS : the filesystem is the source; OPT_MEDIAFIFO: the media FIFO is used for the source

name: EVE_CoCmd_interrupt
signature: void EVE_CoCmd_interrupt(EVE_HalContext *phost, uint32_t ms)
summary: This command is used to trigger Interrupt INT_CMDFLAG

params.name: ms
params.desc: The ms parameter specifies the delay before the interrupt is triggered, measured in milliseconds. If ms set to 0, the interrupt fires immediately

name: EVE_CoCmd_keys
signature: void EVE_CoCmd_keys(EVE_HalContext *phost, int16_t x, int16_t y, int16_t w, int16_t h, uint16_t font, uint16_t options, const char *s)
summary: This command is used to draw a row of keys

params.name: x
params.desc: x-coordinate of keys top-left, in pixels

params.name: y
params.desc: y-coordinate of keys top-left, in pixels

params.name: font
params.desc: Bitmap handle to specify the font used in key label. The valid range is from 0 to 63

params.name: options
params.desc: OPT_3D - default option, the keys are drawn with a 3D effect; OPT_FLAT - removes the 3D effect; OPT_CENTER - the keys are drawn at minimum size centered within the w x h rectangle. Otherwise, the keys are expanded so that they completely fill the available space. If an ASCII code is specified, that key is drawn 'pressed' - i.e. in background color with any 3D effect removed

params.name: s
params.desc: key labels, one character per key. The TAG value is set to the ASCII value of each key, so that key presses can be detected using the REG_TOUCH_TAG register.

name: EVE_CoCmd_loadAsset
signature: void EVE_CoCmd_loadAsset(EVE_HalContext *phost, uint32_t ptr, uint32_t options)
summary: The loadasset command loads an asset in .reloc format at the given address

params.name: ptr
params.desc: Destination address. It must be 64-byte aligned

params.name: options
params.desc: By default, the data is sourced from the command buffer, immediately following the command itself. Other sources can be selected using one of the following options: OPT_FLASH: flash memory is the source. OPT_FS: the filesystem is the source. OPT_MEDIAFIFO: the media FIFO is used for the source. In each case, the source must be explicitly selected using CMD_FLASHSOURCE, CMD_FSSOURCE or CMD_MEDIAFIFO before issuing this command, otherwise behavior is undefined

name: EVE_CoCmd_loadIdentity
signature: void EVE_CoCmd_loadIdentity(EVE_HalContext *phost)
summary: This command instructs the coprocessor engine to set the current matrix to the identity matrix, so that the coprocessor engine can form the new matrix as requested by CMD_SCALE, CMD_ROTATE and CMD_TRANSLATE command

name: EVE_CoCmd_loadImage
signature: void EVE_CoCmd_loadImage(EVE_HalContext *phost, uint32_t ptr, uint32_t options)
summary: This command is used to load a JPEG or PNG image, decompress it into an EVE-specific bitmap in RAM_G, and generate the display list to set up the bitmap handle

params.name: ptr
params.desc: Destination address, where the decompressed bitmap stores

params.name: options
desc: OPT_MONO - loaded in L8 format; OPT_YCBCR - loaded in YCBCR format; OPT_ TRUECOLOR - loaded in ARGB8 format
OPT_FULLSCREEN causes the bitmap to be scaled so that it fills as much of the screen as possible
OPT_FLASH: flash memory is the source; OPT_FS: the filesystem is the source, see CMD_FSSOURCE; OPT_MEDIAFIFO: the media FIFO is used for the source
OPT_DITHER - the image is dithered with a 2x2 ordered dither
OPT_TRUECOLOR - bitmap formats ARGB8 or RGB8 are used where appropriate

name: EVE_CoCmd_loadWav
signature: void EVE_CoCmd_loadWav(EVE_HalContext *phost, uint32_t dst, uint32_t options)
summary: The cmd_loadwav command loads a WAV file into memory so that it can be played or looped asynchronously

params.name: dst
params.desc: Destination address. If -1 is given as the address, then the current allocation pointer is used instead

params.name: options
params.desc: By default, the data is sourced from the command buffer, immediately following the command itself. Other sources can be selected using one of the following options: OPT_FLASH: flash memory is the source; OPT_FS: the filesystem is the source; OPT_MEDIAFIFO: the media FIFO is used for the source. In each case, the source must be explicitly selected using CMD_FLASHSOURCE, CMD_FSSOURCE or CMD_MEDIAFIFO before issuing this command, otherwise behavior is undefined

name: EVE_CoCmd_logo
signature: void EVE_CoCmd_logo(EVE_HalContext *phost)
summary: The logo command causes the coprocessor engine to play back a short animation of the Bridgetek logo

name: EVE_CoCmd_mediaFifo
signature: void EVE_CoCmd_mediaFifo(EVE_HalContext *phost, uint32_t ptr, uint32_t size)
summary: This command is to set up a streaming media FIFO. Allocate the specified area of RAM_G and set it up as streaming media FIFO

params.name: ptr
params.desc: starting address of media FIFO, 4-byte aligned

params.name: size
params.desc: number of bytes of media FIFO, 4-byte aligned

name: EVE_CoCmd_memCpy
signature: void EVE_CoCmd_memCpy(EVE_HalContext *phost, uint32_t dest, uint32_t src, uint32_t num)
summary: This command is used to copy a block of memory

params.name: dest
params.desc: address of the destination memory block

params.name: src
params.desc: address of the source memory block

name: EVE_CoCmd_memCrc
signature: bool EVE_CoCmd_memCrc(EVE_HalContext *phost, uint32_t ptr, uint32_t num, uint32_t *result)
summary: This command computes a CRC-32 for a block of RAM_G memory

params.name: ptr
params.desc: Starting address of the memory block

params.name: num
params.desc: Number of bytes in the source memory block. Supports up to 32-bit values but is limited by the available RAM_G size

params.name: result
params.desc: Output parameter; written with the CRC-32 after command execution

name: EVE_CoCmd_memSet
signature: void EVE_CoCmd_memSet(EVE_HalContext *phost, uint32_t ptr, uint32_t value, uint32_t num)
summary: Send CMD_MEMSET

params.name: ptr
params.desc: Starting address of the memory block

params.name: value
params.desc: Value to be written to memory

params.name: num
params.desc: Number of bytes in the memory block

name: EVE_CoCmd_memWrite
signature: void EVE_CoCmd_memWrite(EVE_HalContext *phost, uint32_t ptr, uint32_t num)
summary: This command writes the following bytes into the memory

params.name: ptr
params.desc: The memory address to be written

params.name: num
params.desc: Number of bytes to be written

name: EVE_CoCmd_memWrite32
signature: void EVE_CoCmd_memWrite32(EVE_HalContext *phost, uint32_t ptr, uint32_t value)
summary: This command writes 4 bytes into the memory, followed by a single 32-bit value.

params.name: ptr
params.desc: The memory address to be written

name: EVE_CoCmd_memZero
signature: void EVE_CoCmd_memZero(EVE_HalContext *phost, uint32_t ptr, uint32_t num)
summary: This command is used to write zero to a block of memory

params.name: ptr
params.desc: starting address of the memory block

params.name: num
params.desc: number of bytes in the memory block

name: EVE_CoCmd_newList
signature: void EVE_CoCmd_newList(EVE_HalContext *phost, uint32_t a)
summary: This command starts the compilation of a command list into RAM_G

params.name: a
params.desc: memory address of start of command list. If -1 is given as the address, then the current allocation pointer is used instead

name: EVE_CoCmd_nop
signature: void EVE_CoCmd_nop(EVE_HalContext *phost)
summary: The nop command does nothing

name: EVE_CoCmd_number
signature: void EVE_CoCmd_number(EVE_HalContext *phost, int16_t x, int16_t y, uint16_t font, uint16_t options, int32_t n)
summary: This command is used to draw a number

params.name: x
params.desc: x-coordinate of text base, in pixels

params.name: y
params.desc: y-coordinate of text base, in pixels

params.name: options
params.desc: By default (x,y) is the top-left pixel of the text. OPT_CENTERX centers the text horizontally; OPT_CENTERY centers it vertically; OPT_CENTER centers the text in both directions; OPT_RIGHTX right-justifies the text, so that the x is the rightmost pixel. OPT_SIGNED - the number is treated as signed and prefixed by a minus sign if negative.

params.name: n
params.desc: The number to display is either unsigned or signed 32-bit, in the base specified in the preceding CMD_SETBASE.If no CMD_SETBASE appears before CMD_NUMBER, it will be in decimal base

name: EVE_CoCmd_playVideo
signature: void EVE_CoCmd_playVideo(EVE_HalContext *phost, uint32_t options)
summary: Plays back MJPEG-encoded AVI video. The playback starts immediately, and the command completes when playback ends. The playback may be paused or terminated by writing to 32-bit register REG_PLAY_CONTROL

params.name: options
desc: OPT_FULLSCREEN: decodes the video frame into the size specified by render target REG_RE_W and REG_REG_H.
OPT_SOUND: decode and play back the audio sound stream of input .AVI file.
OPT_OVERLAY: Append the video bitmap to an existing display list, instead of starting a new display list.
OPT_DIRECT: play back the video directly to the scanout system. (REG_SO_FORMAT must be YCBCR and scanout screen size must match the video size.)
OPT_YCBCR uses YCBCR format bitmaps for video frames. The default is RGB565
OPT_TRUECOLOR uses ARGB8 format bitmaps for video frames. The default is RGB565

OPT_COMPLETEREG: the application should set the register REG_OBJECT_COMPLETE to 1 at the end of the video data. This prevents a hanging with truncated video data in the media FIFO, enabling the EVE to detect this condition and raise an exception

OPT_MEDIAFIFO: instead of sourcing the AVI video data from the command buffer, source it from the media FIFO in RAM_G; OPT_FLASH: Source video data from flash. When flash is the source, call CMD_FLASHSOURCE before this command to specify the address; OPT_FS: the filesystem is the source. In each case, the source must be explicitly selected using CMD_FLASHSOURCE, CMD_FSSOURCE or CMD_MEDIAFIFO before issuing this command, otherwise behavior is undefined

name: EVE_CoCmd_playWav
signature: void EVE_CoCmd_playWav(EVE_HalContext *phost, uint32_t options)
summary: Plays back an audio sample

params.name: options
params.desc: By default, the data is sourced from the command buffer, immediately following the command itself. Other sources can be selected using one of the following options: OPT_MEDIAFIFO: Instead of sourcing the audio data from the command buffer, source it from the media FIFO in RAM_G; OPT_FLASH: Source video data from flash. When flash is the source, call CMD_FLASHSOURCE before this command to specify the address; OPT_FS: The filesystem is the source

name: EVE_CoCmd_progress
signature: void EVE_CoCmd_progress(EVE_HalContext *phost, int16_t x, int16_t y, int16_t w, int16_t h, uint16_t options, uint16_t val, uint16_t range)
summary: This command is used to draw a progress bar

params.name: x
params.desc: x-coordinate of progress bar top-left, in pixels

params.name: y
params.desc: y-coordinate of progress bar top-left, in pixels

params.name: w
params.desc: Width of the progress bar, in pixels

params.name: h
params.desc: Height of the progress bar, in pixels

params.name: options
params.desc: OPT_3D - default option, the progress bar is drawn with a 3D effect; OPT_FLAT - remove the 3D effect

params.name: val
params.desc: Displayed value of progress bar, between 0 and range inclusive

params.name: range
params.desc: Maximum value of the progress bar

name: EVE_CoCmd_regRead
signature: bool EVE_CoCmd_regRead(EVE_HalContext *phost, uint32_t ptr, uint32_t *result)
summary: This command reads a register value

params.name: ptr
params.desc: Address of the register to be read

params.name: result
params.desc: The register value which has been read from the ptr address OUTPUT parameter. Write a dummy 32-bit value 0x00000000 for this parameter and the Co-Processor will replace this value with the result after the command has been executed. After execution, the host should then reads the address of this parameter in RAM_CMD to get the result value

name: EVE_CoCmd_regWrite
signature: void EVE_CoCmd_regWrite(EVE_HalContext *phost, uint32_t ptr, uint32_t value)
summary: This command writes a value to a register. While it is possible to write a value directly to a register, the advantage of using this command is that the write happens in order with other commands

name: EVE_CoCmd_renderTarget
signature: void EVE_CoCmd_renderTarget(EVE_HalContext *phost, uint32_t dst, uint16_t fmt, int16_t w, int16_t h)
summary: This command sets REG_RE_DEST, REG_RE_FORMAT, REG_RE_W, REG_RE_H

params.name: dst
params.desc: Render target start address, either SWAPCHAIN_0 or a 128-byte aligned memory

params.name: fmt
params.desc: Render target format, one of L8, LA8, RGB565, RGB6, RGB8, ARGB4, ARGB1555, ARGB6, ARGB8, YCBCR

params.name: w
params.desc: Render target width in pixels and must be a multiple of 16

params.name: h
params.desc: Render target height in pixels. w x h must be a multiple of 128

name: EVE_CoCmd_resetFonts
signature: void EVE_CoCmd_resetFonts(EVE_HalContext *phost)
summary: This command loads bitmap handles 16-34 with their default fonts

name: EVE_CoCmd_restoreContext
signature: void EVE_CoCmd_restoreContext(EVE_HalContext *phost)
summary: This command adds a RESTORE_CONTEXT to the display list and restores the coprocessor graphics state from the stack

name: EVE_CoCmd_result
signature: void EVE_CoCmd_result(EVE_HalContext *phost, uint32_t dst)
summary: The result command copies the result field of the preceding command into memory. This can make accessing the result value more convenient than reading directly from the command FIFO

name: EVE_CoCmd_return
signature: void EVE_CoCmd_return(EVE_HalContext *phost)
summary: This command ends a command list. Normally it is not needed by the user, because CMD_ENDLIST appends CMD_RETURN to the command list. However, it may be used in situations where the user is constructing command lists offline

name: EVE_CoCmd_romFont
signature: void EVE_CoCmd_romFont(EVE_HalContext *phost, uint32_t font, uint32_t romslot)
summary: This command is to load a ROM font into bitmap handle

name: EVE_CoCmd_rotate
signature: void EVE_CoCmd_rotate(EVE_HalContext *phost, int32_t a)
summary: This command is used to apply a rotation to the current matrix

params.name: a
params.desc: Clockwise rotation angle, in units of 1/65536 of a circle

name: EVE_CoCmd_rotateAround
signature: void EVE_CoCmd_rotateAround(EVE_HalContext *phost, int32_t x, int32_t y, uint32_t a, int32_t s)
summary: This command is used to apply a rotation and scale around a specified coordinate

params.name: a
params.desc: clockwise rotation angle, in units of 1/65536 of a circle

params.name: s
params.desc: scale factor, in signed 16.16 bit fixed-point form

name: EVE_CoCmd_runAnim
signature: void EVE_CoCmd_runAnim(EVE_HalContext *phost, uint32_t waitmask, uint32_t play)
summary: This command is used to play/run animations until complete

params.name: waitmask
params.desc: 32-bit mask specifying which animation channels to wait for. Animation ends when the logical AND of this mask and REG_ANIM_ACTIVE is zero

params.name: play
params.desc: Address of play control byte. Animation stops when the byte at play is not zero. If this feature is not required, the special value of -1 (0xFFFF FFFF) means that there is no control byte

name: EVE_CoCmd_saveContext
signature: void EVE_CoCmd_saveContext(EVE_HalContext *phost)
summary: This command adds a SAVE_CONTEXT to the display list and preserves the coprocessor graphics state on the state stack

name: EVE_CoCmd_scale
signature: void EVE_CoCmd_scale(EVE_HalContext *phost, int32_t sx, int32_t sy)
summary: This command is used to apply a scale to the current matrix

params.name: sx
params.desc: x scale factor, in signed 16.16 bit fixed-point form. This format consists of a 32-bit signed value, where the upper 16 bits represent the integer part and the lower 16 bits represent the fractional part. For example, a value of 1.5 in 16.16 fixed-point format is calculated as 1.5 x 65536 = 98304, which corresponds to the hexadecimal value 0x00018000

params.name: sy
params.desc: y scale factor, in signed 16.16 bit fixed-point form

name: EVE_CoCmd_screenSaver
signature: void EVE_CoCmd_screenSaver(EVE_HalContext *phost)
summary: This command is used to start an animated screensaver

name: EVE_CoCmd_scrollbar
signature: void EVE_CoCmd_scrollbar(EVE_HalContext *phost, int16_t x, int16_t y, int16_t w, int16_t h, uint16_t options, uint16_t val, uint16_t size, uint16_t range)
summary: This command is used to draw a scroll bar

params.name: x
params.desc: x-coordinate of scroll bar top-left, in pixels

params.name: y
params.desc: y-coordinate of scroll bar top-left, in pixels

params.name: w
params.desc: Width of scroll bar, in pixels. If width is greater than height, the scroll bar is drawn horizontally

params.name: h
params.desc: Height of scroll bar, in pixels. If height is greater than width, the scroll bar is drawn vertically

params.name: options
params.desc: OPT_3D - default option, the scroll bar is drawn with a 3D effect; OPT_FLAT - remove the 3D effect

params.name: val
params.desc: Displayed value of scroll bar, between 0 and range inclusive

name: EVE_CoCmd_sdAttach
signature: bool EVE_CoCmd_sdAttach(EVE_HalContext *phost, uint32_t options, uint32_t *result)
summary: This command causes the EVE to connect to the attached SD storage

params.name: options
params.desc: A bitwise-or of the following flags: OPT_4BIT - causes the SD interface to be initialized in 4-bit mode. The default is 1-bit mode. OPT_HALFSPEED - the SD card to run at half-speed (one quarter of thesystem clock). The default is full speed. OPT_QUARTERSPEED - the SD card to run at quarter-speed (one eighth of the system clock). The default is full speed. OPT_IS_SD - the attached storage is an SDcard. OPT_IS_MMC assume the attached storage is eMMC

params.name: result
desc: Written with the result code. If the command succeeds, zero is written as a result

0xd000: SD card is not detected. Check card detect line; 0xd001: no response from card. Probable signal or card problem; 0xd002: card timeout during initialization. Probable problem with card; 0xd003: sector 0 (MBR) not found on card. Is the card blank? 0xd004: FAT filesystem not found. Is the card properly formatted with a FAT filesystem? 0xd005: Card failed to enter high-speed mode

name: EVE_CoCmd_sdBlockread
signature: bool EVE_CoCmd_sdBlockread(EVE_HalContext *phost, uint32_t dst, uint32_t src, uint32_t num, uint32_t *result)
summary: The sdblockread command reads multiple 512-byte blocks from SD into RAM_G

params.name: dst
params.desc: Destination in main memory, must be 4-byte aligned

name: EVE_CoCmd_setBase
signature: void EVE_CoCmd_setBase(EVE_HalContext *phost, uint32_t base)
summary: This command is used to set the base for number output

params.name: base

name: EVE_CoCmd_setBitmap

params.name: width

params.name: height

name: EVE_CoCmd_setFont
signature: void EVE_CoCmd_setFont(EVE_HalContext *phost, uint32_t font, uint32_t ptr, uint32_t firstchar)
summary: This CMD_SETFONT is equivalent to CMD_SETFONT2 in previous generations and is used to set up a custom font

name: EVE_CoCmd_setMatrix

name: EVE_CoCmd_setRotate

name: EVE_CoCmd_sketch

summary: This command supports a single touch point and initiates a continuous sketch update

params.name: x
params.desc: x-coordinate of the sketch area top-left, in pixels

params.name: y
params.desc: y-coordinate of the sketch area top-left, in pixels

params.name: w
params.desc: Width of the sketch area, in pixels

params.name: h
params.desc: Height of the sketch area, in pixels

params.name: format
params.desc: Format of sketch bitmap, either L1 or L8

name: EVE_CoCmd_skipCond
signature: void EVE_CoCmd_skipCond(EVE_HalContext *phost, uint32_t a, uint32_t func, uint32_t ref, uint32_t mask, uint32_t num)
summary: Skip following command bytes if a given condition is true

params.name: a
params.desc: Address of RAM_G or register to check, 32-bit aligned

params.name: func
params.desc: one of NEVER, LESS, LEQUAL, GREATER, GEQUAL, EQUAL, NOTEQUAL or ALWAYS

params.name: num
params.desc: Number of command bytes to skip. If the commands are sourced from the command fifo, this must be positive. If sourced from a calllist, it may be negative

name: EVE_CoCmd_slider
signature: void EVE_CoCmd_slider(EVE_HalContext *phost, int16_t x, int16_t y, int16_t w, int16_t h, uint16_t options, uint16_t val, uint16_t range)
summary: This command is to draw a slider

params.name: x
params.desc: x-coordinate of slider top-left, in pixels

params.name: y
params.desc: y-coordinate of slider top-left, in pixels

params.name: w
params.desc: width of slider, in pixels. If width is greater than height, the scroll bar is drawn horizontally

params.name: h
params.desc: height of slider, in pixels. If height is greater than width, the scroll bar is drawn vertically
